title: zapoteca portoricensis subsp. flavida
intro: Zapoteca portoricensis subsp. flavida, also known as Yellow Zapote, is a plant species native to the Caribbean region, particularly in Puerto Rico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The Yellow Zapote is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help protect against chronic diseases and promote overall well-being. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves and bark can also be used to make extracts and tinctures for various health purposes.
